articles.title: BKA Identifies REvil Leaders Behind 130 German Ransomware Attacks
articles.summary: Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (aka BKA or the Bundeskriminalamt) has unmasked the real identity of the main threat actors associated with the now-defunct REvil  (aka Sodinokibi ) ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation.  The threat actor, who went by the alias UNKN , functioned as a representative of the group, advertising the ransomware in June 2019 on the XSS cybercrime forum. He has now been identified as Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin , a 31-year-old Russian national. He also went by the online monikers Oneiilk2, Oneillk2, Oneillk22, and GandCrab.  The development was reported  by independent security journalist Brian Krebs.  "From early 2019 at the latest until at least July 2021, the wanted person, in cooperation with other individuals, acted as the leader of one of the largest global ransomware groups, known as GandCrab/REvil," BKA said. "The perpetrators demanded large ransom payments in exchange for decrypting and not leaking da...
articles.title_fr: Le BKA identifie les leaders de REvil derrière 130 attaques de rançongiciels en Allemagne
articles.summary_fr: Le Bundeskriminalamt (BKA) allemand a révélé l'identité des principaux acteurs de la menace derrière l'opération de rançongiciel en tant que service (RaaS) REvil, désormais inactive. Le principal acteur, connu sous l'alias UNKN, a été identifié comme étant Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin, un ressortissant russe de 31 ans. Il aurait dirigé l'un des plus grands groupes mondiaux de rançongiciels, GandCrab/REvil, de début 2019 à juillet 2021.

articles.title: $285 Million Drift Hack Traced to Six-Month DPRK Social Engineering Operation
articles.summary: Drift has revealed  that the April 1, 2026, attack that led to the theft of $285 million  was the culmination of a months-long targeted and meticulously planned social engineering operation undertaken by the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) that began in the fall of 2025.  The Solana-based decentralized exchange described it as "an attack six months in the making," attributing it with medium confidence to a North Korean state-sponsored hacking group dubbed UNC4736 , which is also tracked under the cyptonyms AppleJeus, Citrine Sleet, Golden Chollima, and Gleaming Pisces.  The threat actor has a history  of targeting the cryptocurrency sector for financial theft since at least 2018. It's best known for the X_TRADER/3CX supply chain breach  in 2023 and the $53 million hack  of decentralized finance (DeFi) platform Radiant Capital  in October 2024.  "The basis for this connection is both on-chain (f...
articles.title_fr: Le piratage de 285 millions de dollars de Drift attribué à une opération d'ingénierie sociale nord-coréenne de six mois
articles.summary_fr: La plateforme Drift a révélé que le vol de 285 millions de dollars, survenu le 1er avril 2026, était l'aboutissement d'une opération d'ingénierie sociale de six mois menée par la Corée du Nord. L'attaque, débutée à l'automne 2025, est attribuée au groupe de hackers parrainé par l'État nord-coréen UNC4736, connu pour cibler le secteur des cryptomonnaies depuis 2018.

articles.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy reverse shells, harvest credentials, and drop a persistent implant.  "Every package contains three files (package.json, index.js, postinstall.js), has no description, repository, or homepage, and uses version 3.6.8 to appear as a mature Strapi v3 community plugin," SafeDep said .  All identified npm packages follow the same naming convention, starting with "strapi-plugin-" and then phrases like "cron," "database," or "server" to fool unsuspecting developers into downloading them. It's worth noting that the official Strapi plugins are scoped under "@strapi/."  The packages, uploaded by four sock puppet accounts "umarbek1233," "kekylf12," "tikeqemif26," and "umar_bektembiev1...
articles.title_fr: 36 paquets npm malveillants ont exploité Redis et PostgreSQL pour déployer des implants persistants
articles.summary_fr: Des chercheurs ont découvert 36 paquets malveillants dans le registre npm, déguisés en plugins Strapi CMS. Ces paquets exploitent Redis et PostgreSQL, déploient des shells inversés, récoltent des identifiants et installent des implants persistants. Ils utilisaient des conventions de nommage spécifiques et la version 3.6.8 pour tromper les développeurs.
